2605.07301.title: SOM: Structured Opponent Modeling for LLM-based Agents via Structural Causal Model
2605.07301.paper_summary: Accurately predicting opponents' behavior from interactions is a fundamental capability for large language model (LLM)-based agents in multi-agent and game-theoretic environments. Existing approaches often entangle opponent modeling with prediction, relying on implicit contextual reasoning and limiting adaptability in dynamic interactions. To this end, we propose Structured Opponent Modeling (SOM), a two-stage opponent modeling framework that distinctly separates opponent model construction and opponent prediction. At the construction stage, SOM employs a Structural Causal Model (SCM), a graph-based formalism for representing dependencies among variables, to capture directed links between opponents' observations and actions, yielding an explicit and structured opponent representation. At the prediction stage, the LLM performs structured reasoning along clear pathways derived from the SCM, improving both prediction accuracy and stability. Extensive experiments on diverse multi-agent benchmarks demonstrate that SOM consistently outperforms state-of-the-art LLM-based reasoning baselines, enabling more accurate and adaptable strategic decision-making in complex and dynamic multi-agent interactions.
2605.07301.paper_summary_zh: 準確預測對手在互動中的行為是基於大型語言模型（LLM）代理在多代理和博弈理論環境中的基本能力。現有的方法通常將對手建模與預測糾纏在一起，依賴隱式的上下文推理，並限制了在動態互動中的適應性。為此，我們提出了結構化對手建模（SOM），這是一個兩階段的對手建模框架，明確區分對手模型的構建和對手的預測。在構建階段，SOM採用結構因果模型（SCM），這是一種基於圖的形式主義，用於表示變量之間的依賴關係，以捕捉對手觀察和行為之間的有向連結，從而產生明確且結構化的對手表示。在預測階段，LLM沿著從SCM衍生出的清晰路徑進行結構化推理，提高了預測的準確性和穩定性。在多樣化的多代理基準上進行的廣泛實驗表明，SOM始終超越最先進的基於LLM的推理基準，從而在複雜和動態的多代理互動中實現更準確和更具適應性的戰略決策。
2605.07301.author: Shiyue Cao et.al.
2605.07301.authors: Shiyue Cao, Pei Xu, Likun Yang, Lei Cui, Xiaotang Chen, Kaiqi Huang
